title: Add minimal spatial audio at the hosted CUA root
summary: 0.6.69 adds minimal single-source spatial audio by routing the player AudioSource through a dedicated anchor at the hosted CUA root while keeping the existing playback runtime and transport model intact.
changes: Hosted player records now create a dedicated audio anchor at the hosted CUA root and route VideoPlayer audio into an AudioSource on that anchor instead of the private runtime sandbox object.
The player audio source now uses spatial settings with doppler still disabled, conservative logarithmic falloff, and a small near-field distance.
The render/runtime object, video path, transport, sync, slideshow, and plugin UI remain unchanged in this slice.
reasoning: The repo already carries a single AudioSource output path, so the smallest spatial-audio seam is to keep that path but move only the audio anchor to the hosted CUA root. That avoids widening into speaker/profile architecture or moving the whole playback sandbox while still proving spatial behavior in the live hosted-player seam.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Hosted player audio now comes from the hosted CUA root instead of behaving as flat 2D sound.
Playback controls, sync, slideshow, image navigation, and the current runtime object behavior stay the same.
knownIssues: This slice does not add a spatial-audio UI control or profile system.
This slice does not change multi-speaker architecture, transport behavior, or default-preset autoload.
verification: FASyncRuntime.Player.cs now keeps runtimeObject under runtimeRoot but creates a dedicated audio anchor for the AudioSource at the hosted CUA root.
The AudioSource is configured for spatial output while VideoPlayer continues targeting that source through the existing audio routing seam.
